description:
You are an observability engineer reviewing a set of **already-collected** network-diagnostic command dumps that live on the host.  
    Your goal is to create a pair of easily-ingestible summary artefacts for a dashboarding system.

    Starting state
    1. A directory named /home/user/network_diagnostics/raw already exists.
    2. It contains three plain-text files that hold the verbatim outputs of common
       network-diagnostic tools:
          • /home/user/network_diagnostics/raw/ping_localhost.txt  
          • /home/user/network_diagnostics/raw/traceroute_localhost.txt  
          • /home/user/network_diagnostics/raw/ss_output.txt  

       (These files are supplied for you – **do NOT edit them**.)

    Task – create a summarised view
    1. Make a new directory called
         /home/user/network_diagnostics/2023-obs-summary
       (create any missing leading directories as needed).
    2. Read /home/user/network_diagnostics/raw/ping_localhost.txt and extract the
       following numeric items:
         • packets transmitted
         • packets received
         • packet-loss percentage (exclude the ‘%’ sign in the numeric value)
         • rtt minimum in ms
         • rtt average in ms
         • rtt maximum in ms
    3. Read /home/user/network_diagnostics/raw/ss_output.txt and count how many
       **LISTEN** TCP sockets are shown (i.e. lines whose “State” column is
       “LISTEN” and whose “Netid” column is “tcp”).
    4. Produce TWO new files inside
         /home/user/network_diagnostics/2023-obs-summary

       a) summary.csv  
          • MUST be exactly eight (8) lines long.  
          • Line-1 is a header:  
                metric,value  
          • Lines 2-8 hold the extracted values in this order (each on its own
            line, no extra whitespace):  
                packets_transmitted,<integer>  
                packets_received,<integer>  
                packet_loss_percent,<integer>  
                rtt_min_ms,<float with 3 decimal places>  
                rtt_avg_ms,<float with 3 decimal places>  
                rtt_max_ms,<float with 3 decimal places>  
                tcp_listening_sockets,<integer>  

       b) summary.json  
          • MUST be valid JSON (no trailing commas).  
          • The JSON object must contain exactly the seven keys shown above,
            using the identical spellings.  
          • Numbers must appear as numbers, not strings.  
          • Keys may appear in any order.

    5. When everything is done, list (ls ‑R or tree) the entire
       /home/user/network_diagnostics directory so the grader can see the final
       structure, then display (cat) the two new files so their contents are
       visible in the console log of your session.

    Notes / rules
    • Do NOT hardcode the numerical answers; they must be parsed from the raw
      files.  
    • Do NOT delete, rename or move the three raw files.  
    • You do NOT need root privileges for any step.  
    • Absolute reproducibility is required: the grader will compare the two
      summary files byte-for-byte against the specification above.
truth:
<!--
    Files that exist before the agent begins
    -->
    <pre_existing_files>
        <file path="/home/user/network_diagnostics/raw/ping_localhost.txt"><![CDATA[PING 127.0.0.1 (127.0.0.1) 56(84) bytes of data.
64 bytes from 127.0.0.1: icmp_seq=1 ttl=64 time=0.026 ms
64 bytes from 127.0.0.1: icmp_seq=2 ttl=64 time=0.031 ms
64 bytes from 127.0.0.1: icmp_seq=3 ttl=64 time=0.025 ms
64 bytes from 127.0.0.1: icmp_seq=4 ttl=64 time=0.035 ms

--- 127.0.0.1 ping statistics ---
4 packets transmitted, 4 received, 0% packet loss, time 3058ms
rtt min/avg/max/mdev = 0.025/0.029/0.035/0.004 ms
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/network_diagnostics/raw/traceroute_localhost.txt"><![CDATA[traceroute to 127.0.0.1 (127.0.0.1), 30 hops max, 60 byte packets
 1  localhost (127.0.0.1) 0.031 ms 0.014 ms 0.012 ms
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/network_diagnostics/raw/ss_output.txt"><![CDATA[Netid  State      Recv-Q Send-Q Local Address:Port               Peer Address:Port              
udp    UNCONN     0      0              0.0.0.0:68                          0.0.0.0:*      users:(("dhclient",pid=658,fd=6))                                                    
tcp    LISTEN     0      128            0.0.0.0:22                          0.0.0.0:*      users:(("sshd",pid=744,fd=3))                                                      
tcp    LISTEN     0      128                  *:80                                *:*      users:(("nginx",pid=800,fd=4))                                                    
]]></file>
    </pre_existing_files>

    <!--
    Files that must exist AFTER the agent finishes
    -->
    <expected_output_files>

        <file path="/home/user/network_diagnostics/2023-obs-summary/summary.csv"><![CDATA[metric,value
packets_transmitted,4
packets_received,4
packet_loss_percent,0
rtt_min_ms,0.025
rtt_avg_ms,0.029
rtt_max_ms,0.035
tcp_listening_sockets,2
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/network_diagnostics/2023-obs-summary/summary.json"><![CDATA|{
  "packets_transmitted": 4,
  "packets_received": 4,
  "packet_loss_percent": 0,
  "rtt_min_ms": 0.025,
  "rtt_avg_ms": 0.029,
  "rtt_max_ms": 0.035,
  "tcp_listening_sockets": 2
}|]]></file>
    </expected_output_files>
